entries.definition:
brought into the open.
يُبْلِي yublī [imperf. of v. IV أَبْلَى ʾablā trans.] either to give the chance to someone to do well or to confer a great gift on (8:17) وَلِيُبْلِيَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْهُ بَلَاء حَسَنًا and to test the believers with a fair test from Him (or, to confer upon the believers a great gift from Him).

اِبْتَلَى ʾibtalā [v. VIII, trans.] 1 to test (2:124) وَإِذِ ابْتَلَى إِبْرَهِيمَ رَبُّهُ بِكَلِمَاتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ and when his Lord tested Abraham by certain words/commands and he fulfilled them 2 to put to the test (89:15) فَأَمَّا الْإِنسَانُ إِذَا مَا ابْتَلَاهُ رَبُّهُ فَأَكْرَمَهُ وَنَعَّمَهُ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّي أَكْرَمَنِ as for man, whenever his Lord puts him to the test, honouring him and granting him ease in living—he says ‘My Lord has honoured me’ 3 to confer (8:17) وَلِيُبْلِيَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْهُ بَلَاء حَسَنًا to confer upon the believers a great gift from Him (or, and to test the believers with a fair test from Him).

اِبْتُلِيَ ʾibtuliya [pass. of v. VIII] 1 to test, to be put to the test (33:11) هُنَالِكَ ابْتُلِيَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَزُلْزِلُوا زِلْزَالًا شَدِيدًا there the believers were sorely tested and deeply shaken.

بَلَاء balāʾ [n./v. n.] 1 test, trial (44:33) وَآتَيْنَاهُم مِّنَ الْآيَاتِ مَا فِيهِ بَلَاء مُبِينٌ We gave them revelations in which there was a clear test; *(8:17) بَلَاء حَسَنًا favour, blessing, great benefit [lit. a good test] 2 affliction (2:49) يَذْبَحُونَ أَبْنَاءكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَاءكُمْ وَفِي ذَلِكُم بَلَاء مِن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ slaughtering your sons and sparing only your women, this was a terrible affliction from your Lord.

مُبْتَلِي mubtalī [act. part., pl. مُبْتَلِين mubtalīn] one who puts to the test (2:249) إِنَّ اللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍ God will test you with a river.

مُبْتَلِي mubtalī [act. part., pl. مُبْتَلِين mubtalīn] one who puts to the test (2:249) إِنَّ اللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍ God will test you with a river.

entries.definition:
b–l–y to be worn out, to degenerate with age, to whither away; to care about, to give attention to. This root is usually classified together with root ب/ل/و b–l–w (see ب/ي/ل b–y–l). Of this root, يَبْلَى yablā occurs once in the Qur’an; also بَلَى balā occurs 27 times and may be classified under this root.
يَبْلَى yablā [imperf. of v. بَلِيَ baliya, intrans.] to decay, wear out, to wane (20:120) هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى شَجَرَةِ الْخُلْدِ وَمُلْكٍ لَّا يَبْلَى shall I show you the tree of immortality and a kingdom that never wanes?

115
entries.definition_english:
b–l–y to be worn out, to degenerate with age, to whither away; to care about, to give attention to. This root is usually classified together with root ب/ل/و b–l–w (see ب/ي/ل b–y–l). Of this root, يَبْلَى yablā occurs once in the Qur’an; also بَلَى balā occurs 27 times and may be classified under this root.
يَبْلَى yablā [imperf. of v. بَلِيَ baliya, intrans.] to decay, wear out, to wane (20:120) هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى شَجَرَةِ الْخُلْدِ وَمُلْكٍ لَّا يَبْلَى shall I show you the tree of immortality and a kingdom that never wanes?

115
